name: sql-injection-detection
description: Detects SQL injection via string concatenation in raw pgx queries
trap: Model focuses on other issues (missing LIMIT, SELECT *) but misses the CRITICAL SQL injection vulnerability

assertions.text: Flags SQL injection as CRITICAL severity
Identifies string concatenation with user input as the root cause
Suggests parameterized query with $1 placeholder as the fix

name: nil-dereference-after-repo-nil-nil
description: Catches nil pointer dereference when repo returns (nil, nil) for not found
trap: Model doesn't notice that when repo returns (nil, nil), the code dereferences vocab.ID on a nil pointer

assertions.text: Flags nil dereference as HIGH severity
Explains that repo returns (nil, nil) for not found — err is nil but vocab is also nil
Suggests adding 'if vocab == nil { return nil, apperr.NotFound(...) }' check after error check

name: missing-transaction-multi-write
description: Detects multiple writes without transaction wrapper risking partial success
trap: Model focuses on the loop pattern (N+1) but misses that create + add items are not wrapped in a transaction

assertions.text: Flags missing transaction as HIGH severity
Explains risk: if Add fails mid-loop, collection exists with partial items

name: apperror-in-repository
description: Catches AppError usage inside repository layer — repos must return raw errors
trap: Model approves the code because the error handling looks correct — but misses that repos must never return AppError

assertions.text: Flags AppError in repository as HIGH severity (architecture violation)
Explains the convention: repos return (nil, nil) for not found, raw error otherwise
Use case is responsible for wrapping into AppError

name: business-logic-in-handler
description: Detects business logic leaking into the handler layer
trap: Model only flags minor style issues but misses that the handler is doing ownership check, repo call, and field mutation — all business logic

assertions.text: Flags handler calling repo directly as MEDIUM architecture violation
Flags ownership check in handler — should be in use case
Flags field mutation (existing.Name = ...) in handler — should be in use case or domain method
Suggests handler should only: bind input, call useCase.UpdateCollection(ctx, userID, collectionID, req), send response

name: n-plus-one-query
description: Detects N+1 query pattern in a loop
trap: Model approves the code as correct — misses the N+1 performance problem

assertions.text: Flags N+1 query as MEDIUM performance issue
Explains: 1 query for topics + N queries for counts = N+1 round trips
Suggests batch query (e.g. BatchCountVocabulary) or JOIN to get counts in a single query
